programme.desc: Cik Som merupakan orang tengah yang mengatur jodoh antara Hanisa dengan Faizal dan juga Suhaimi dengan Marhayu. Cik Som bukan sahaja menjual jamu dan barang kemas, dia juga menjual kambing. Tetapi yang lebih diutamakan Cik Som adalah pencari jodoh. Semuanya itu dilakukan secara percuma dan Cik Som sangat gembira dengan kepercayaan yang diberi kepadanyan. programme.title: Younger
programme.desc: Cerita mengisahkan Liza Miller, seorang ibu tunggal berusia 40 tahun yang mahu kembali bekerja dalam industri penerbitan buku selepas sekian lama berhenti. Disebabkan usianya dianggap 'sudah tua' untuk industri tersebut, dia menyamar sebagai wanita berusia 26 tahun untuk bekerja. Penipuan itu membawa Liza ke pelbagai konflik dari segi kerjaya, percintaan hingga persahabatan sambil cuba mengekalkan rahsianya.

programme.title: Mudhal Mariyathai
programme.desc: Kisah mengenai seorang lelaki berusia bernama Malaichami, seorang ketua kampung yang dihormati tetapi terperangkap dalam perkahwinan yang tidak bahagia dengan isterinya yang kasar dan materialistik. Walaupun hidupnya kelihatan sempurna di mata masyarakat, dia sebenarnya kesepian dan tertekan secara emosional. Kehidupan Malaichami berubah apabila dia mula menjalinkan persahabatan dengan seorang wanita muda dan miskin bernama Kuyil, seorang yang ceria. Walaupun terdapat jurang usia dan perbezaan status sosial, hubungan mereka berkembang menjadi ikatan emosi yang mendalam dan telus.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: GRAND SUMO Highlights
programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from July 24, Day 13 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Nagoya.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with information on the techniques, traditions and famous wrestlers of the past. The rules may be simple but there is a lot more to sumo than meets the eye.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Asia Insight
programme.desc: Climate change has seen vegetable harvests fall in South Korea. One group of women is embracing hardy organic techniques, while the government is training a new generation of farmers with technology.

programme.title: NEWSROOM TOKYO
programme.desc: NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: Yoshida Katsuji investigates unexplored caves around the world, and won the Uemura Naomi Adventure Award for his achievements in Laos. He talks about the appeal of cave exploration.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climate Change the World
programme.desc: Each year, 20 million people are displaced by climate change. If nothing is done, these "climate refugees" will number 216 million by 2050. We examine footage of problem areas and the latest research.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: A Spring Party
programme.desc: We visit one of the most famous cherry blossom spots in Edo. Flower viewing parties were for eating, drinking and making merry. It was also an opportunity for young men and women to mingle.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.desc: A large-scale study in Japan has shown that improving one's diet and lifestyle can slow the progression of chronic kidney disease. Clinical trials in regenerative medicine are also underway.

programme.title: JAPAN DELISH
programme.desc: Japanese festival favorite, yakisoba! Noodles cooked in a special sauce. Discover its origins, little known even in Japan.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: Today's theme: Bringing Dolls to Life. To ward off evil, to celebrate a birth, or simply to comfort and entertain - dolls have a long history in Japan. We meet creators bringing those dolls to life.

programme.desc: Dig deeper into current affairs. Cutting-edge information and critical analysis on important issues confronting Japanese lives.

programme.title: From Pollution to Solution
programme.desc: Cahyo Laksono is passionate about carbon neutrality. The PhD student sees an opportunity to reuse carbon dioxide emissions in Japan and his native Indonesia to help produce plastics.

programme.desc: Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: non-loosening bolts.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.

programme.title: SAMURAI WISDOM
programme.desc: Clad in shining gold, Kinkakuji stands out even in Kyoto. What inspired the samurai who built this stunning temple?

programme.title: CINEMATIC JOURNEY
programme.desc: Kyle Card hikes along the 1000km trail following the Pacific Coast in northern Japanwhere he discovers local lifestyles, stunning nature, traces of the area's 2011 disaster, and resilient people.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: Riverside Sawmill
programme.desc: Workers at a sawmill use the latest tools and their honed skills to prepare the building blocks of Edo life: long, narrow beams, bamboo stems and leftover wood stacked as high as buildings.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: BOSAI
programme.desc: In summer, accidents near the beach are a constant occurrence. But new technologies using smart devices are helping to prevent disaster in Japan.

programme.title: DESIGN MUSEUM JAPAN
programme.desc: Yamagata / Minagawa Akira (designer) To make a "dantsu" carpet, everything from yarn-making to weaving is done by hand. Minagawa experiences the deep allure of handmade goods.

programme.title: GRAND SUMO Highlights
programme.desc: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a sumo highlights program for fans around the globe. Today the show features all top-division bouts from July 25, Day 14 of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Nagoya.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with information on the techniques, traditions and famous wrestlers of the past. The rules may be simple but there is a lot more to sumo than meets the eye.

programme.desc: The final year of WWII. Germany and Japan are locked in their wars of attrition, and the Allied powers are driven to the brink. Humanity opens the gates of hell.

programme.desc: Spaces under Japan's elevated train tracks are being used in a variety of ways, from restaurants to skate parks. Some towns are utilizing these spaces as part of their regional revitalization efforts.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE

programme.title: I Love 'Mee'
programme.desc: Among Mee's friends, there's a popular game where you sneak up and squeeze your friends' butts. When someone does it to Mee, Mee shivers and feels weird. Papa says "That's your Inner Alarm's alert."
